english: a finger Cs. — mdzub-żú (mdzub zhu) thimble Cs. — *dzug-ri* (*dzug ri*) W. = mdzub-brdá (mdzub brda), *dzug-ri-táṅ-cė* (*dzug ri tang ce*) to beckon. — mdzub-śúbs (mdzub shubs) a fingered glove Sch
Ssk. कुष्ठ, leprosy (not cancer, yet infectious, the skin growing white and chapped) Glr., Med.; mdzé-càn (mdze can) leprous.
knot, excrescence of the skin, wart etc. Med.; rus-mdzér (rus mdzer) S.g. bony excrescence, exostosis (?); knag, knot, in wood Dzl.; mdzer-mál (mdzer mal) knot-hole, in boards.

fair, handsome, beautiful, mdzés-pai (mdzes pa'i) or -mai (-ma'i) bú-mo (bu mo) Glr.; bú-mo (bu mo) mdzés-pa (mdzes pa) as a tender address to a daughter Glr.; ri-bo (ri bo) nags-tsál (nags tshal) dú-mas (du mas) mdzés-pa (mdzes pa) a mountain beautified by numerous woods; mdzés-par (mdzes par) byá-bai (bya ba'i) p̃yir (phyir) for show, serving as finery, ornament Stg.; fig.: spyód-lam (spyod lam) mdzés-pa (mdzes pa) a deportment outwardly unblamable Dzl.; lus-mdzés (lus mdzes) a well-made body, ydoṅ-mdzés (gdong mdzes) a handsome face, mig-mdzes (mig mdzes) a beautiful eye Cs.; mdzes-mdzés (mdzes mdzes) pomp, extravagance, profusion, debauchery Sch. — ynod-mdzés (gnod mdzes) name of the rig-sṅags-kyi (rig sngags kyi) rgyál-po (rgyal po) (?) Dom., Lex.

mongrel-breed of the yak-bull and common cow Lt., whilst ₒbri-mdzo ('bri mdzo) (W. *brim-dzo* (*brim dzo*)) is the hybrid of a common bull and a yak-cow, mdzó-p̃o (mdzo pho) a male, mdzó-mo (mdzo mo) a female animal of the kind, both valued as domestic cattle; mdzó-mo-k̃yu (mdzo mo khyu) a herd of such animals; mdzo-rgód (mdzo rgod) wild cattle; mdzo-p̃rúg (mdzo phrug) calf of such cattle; mdzo-kó (mdzo ko) leather, mdzo-már (mdzo mar) butter from a bastard cow, mdzo-sgál (mdzo sgal) load for the same Cs.; mdzo-tsá (mdzo tsha) Wdn. n. of a medicine (cf. ba-tsá? (ba tsha?)).

Ssk. कोष, 1. sbst. store-house, magazine, depository, strong-box, mdzód-du (mdzod du) ₒjúg-pa ('jug pa), sbéd-pa (sbed pa) to secure, to hide a thing in a depository, mdzód-nas (mdzod nas) ₒdón-pa ('don pa) to fetch forth from it; dkor-mdzód (dkor mdzod), yter-mdzód (gter mdzod) Glr. treasury; baṅ-mdzód (bang mdzod) corn-magazine, granary; dbyig-mdzód (dbyig mdzod) a safe for valuables, yser-mdzód (gser mdzod) for gold; p̃yag-mdzód (phyag mdzod) (Cs. also mdzód-pa (mdzod pa)) treasurer, with kings, in large monasteries; miṅ-gi (ming gi) mdzod a treasury of words, dictionary. — mdzod-k̃aṅ (mdzod khang) store-room, larder. — mdzod-srúṅ (mdzod srung) treasurer Dzl. — 2. vb. v. mdzád-pa (mdzad pa).

Ssk. ऊर्णा, smin-mtsams-kyi (smin mtshams kyi) mdzód-spu (mdzod spu) Glr., acc. to Cs. a single hair, acc. to the majority, a circle of hair, between the eye-brows, in the middle of the forehead, one of the particular marks of a Buddha, from which, e.g., he is able to send forth magic or divine rays of light.
Lex.; Sch.: 'grief, dejection; a snare, a trap'(?).
1. exchange, agio C. — 2. interest or premium paid for the use of money borrowed Lh.
prob. only in the word čud-ₒdza-ba (chud 'dza' ba) to be expended in vain Cs. (?).

pf. (y)zags ((g)zags), fut. yzag (gzag), (intrs. to ₒtsag-pa ('tshag pa)), to drop, drip, trickle, sna-k̃rág (sna khrag), sna-ču (sna chu) ₒdzag ('dzag) blood, water, dripping from the nose Med.; *ṅal-ṭag (ngal thag) zág-cė* (*zag ce*) the menstrual flow of females (plain expression for it) W.; mči-ma (mchi ma) Dzl.; ₒó-ma ('o ma) ₒdzág-pa ('dzag pa) dé-las (de las) byuṅ (byung) milk is trickling from it Wdn.; ₒdzag-ₒdzág-pa ('dzag 'dzag pa) to trickle constantly Sch.; in a more gen. sense: to flow out spouting; k̃rag (khrag) yzágs-pa (gzags pa) the blood that has been shed Dzl.; mtso (mtsho) źabs-nas (zhabs nas) zágs-te (zags te) méd-par (med par) soṅ (song) flowing off at the bottom, the lake dwindled away Mil.; *k̃a-ču (kha chu) zug (zug) dug* (dug*) W. he foams (with rage); bźin (bzhin) zags-te (zags te) the face dripping (with perspiration); *śú-gu (shu gu) zags (zags) soṅ* (song*) W. the paper runs, blots: sometimes used transitively: kún-la (kun la) sñiṅ-btse (snying btse) mči-ma (mchi ma) yzag (gzag) he is shedding tears of universal pity Dzl. 22, 16; sor (sor) bar-nas (bar nas) ₒdzág-nas ('dzag nas) letting (the ashes) fall through between his fingers Mil.
mixed, mingled, promiscuously, pell-mell Lexx. = k̃rúgs-pa (khrugs pa).
Lex. = zád-pa (zad pa) spent, consumed, exhausted, construed with nor, of rare occurrence.
